prd.name: Simulator Pick
prd.overview: The `sim pick` command creates a managed iOS simulator for the current worktree and records it in simulator.toml. It supports interactive selection of device type and runtime, with non-interactive overrides and an auto-select default for headless environments.

requirement.name: Device type selection
requirement.description: The picker offers a curated list of iPhone device types and lets the user opt out of the curation for older or specialized devices.
requirement.rule: By default the picker lists only the latest iPhone generation available on the host, so the interactive prompt stays short.
Passing `--all-devices` lifts the curation and lists every iPhone device type the host knows about.
Passing `--device <name>` selects that device type directly and skips the interactive prompt for device choice.

requirement.name: Runtime selection
requirement.description: The picker offers available iOS runtimes and lets the user override interactively or via flag.
requirement.rule: When the user does not specify a runtime, the newest available iOS runtime is offered first.
Passing `--runtime <name>` selects that runtime directly and skips the interactive prompt for runtime choice.

bug.current: The default device-type filter is hardcoded to match only names containing "iPhone 17". When the host's newest iPhone generation is anything other than 17 (older host without iPhone 17 runtimes installed, or a future host with iPhone 18+), the default interactive picker shows an empty or stale list. Users must discover and pass `--all-devices` or `--device` to recover.
bug.expected: The default filter should dynamically select the newest iPhone generation present on the host (by parsing the generation number from device-type names), so the picker remains short and useful without code changes when Apple ships a new generation or when running on older hosts.
bug.steps: 1. Run `worktree-ios-dev-tool sim pick` on a host without any "iPhone 17" device types installed (e.g. only iPhone 16 simulators present). 2. Observe: the interactive device list is empty; the command fails or stalls. 3. Workaround: re-run with `--all-devices` or `--device "iPhone 16 Pro"`.
